title: oenothera neomexicana
intro: Oenothera neomexicana, also known as New Mexico evening primrose, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It is a member of the evening primrose family and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health issues. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers have been used in herbal preparations to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Oenothera neomexicana is often used in herbal remedies in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be prepared by steeping the plant's parts in hot water or a solvent.
